sanitization.policy: fail-closed publishable transcript: hidden reasoning and tool payloads are excluded; credentials, tokens, SSH material, IP addresses, private local paths, environment values, and sensitive infrastructure details are redacted or omitted.
sanitization.uncertainty: medium; provider outputs and launch commands contained infrastructure identifiers, so publishable text records scientific state and redacted operational facts only

ledger: An existing authenticated container-registry entry was used for the next live attempts, fixing the earlier anonymous image-pull/startup problem without changing the registered scientific design.
Candidate one, cumulative_baseline, completed the 8-epoch short stage for COBA and PING with one live pod at a time.
COBA selected epoch 8 with validation accuracy 60.049019607843135%, validation cross-entropy 1.5872331674282367, final E rate 28.346392463235293 Hz, no skipped steps, and no non-finite batches.

PING selected epoch 8 with validation accuracy 57.96568627450981%, validation cross-entropy 1.4047367160136883, final E/I rates 7.494308172487745 / 36.273169424019606 Hz, no skipped steps, and no non-finite batches.
Matched validation curves, activity curves, and input/E/I rasters were published under artifacts/data/exp071.
The active pod list was empty after both collections.

Exact provider billing had not reconciled at checkpoint time. The temporary timestamp-based estimate is 0.77 USD total across killed startup attempts, productive COBA/PING runs, and collector pods; exact spend remains pending.
Candidate one clears the finite-active-above-chance short viability gate. The next registered step is candidate two, cumulative_256_256, before any forty-epoch promotion decision.

messages.content: The earlier hangup is pinned down: anonymous GHCR pulls were the likely culprit. I found the existing registry auth entry, confirmed the active pod list is empty, and I’m re-running the COBA short screen with authenticated image pull — still one pod only.
Much better: the authenticated COBA pod is running and visible. I’m polling it in short windows; if it finishes normally I’ll collect/reap and then start PING.
COBA finished and self-terminated. I’m collecting the artifacts now; the collector itself is transient cloud compute, so I’ll also verify it reaps before launching PING.

COBA short is collected: selected epoch 8 at 60.05% validation accuracy, finite/active, no skipped or non-finite updates, matched rasters present. Active pods are empty again, so I’m starting PING short now under the same authenticated path.
PING is running cleanly now. I’ve also logged the COBA→PING handoff on PR #55. Next step is collection, then I’ll publish both curves/rasters into exp071 before moving to candidate two.

PING finished and collected. Candidate one is now a real short-screen result: COBA 60.05%, PING 57.97%, both finite/active, both with rasters, no skipped/non-finite updates. I’m publishing those artifacts into the report and recording the spend as exact-provider-pending with the conservative timestamp estimate.
